text: Approximately 56% of athletes have vitamin D inadequacy (serum 25(OH)D below 32 ng/mL).
The risk of vitamin D inadequacy increases significantly during winter and spring seasons compared to summer and fall.
Indoor sport athletes have a significantly higher risk of vitamin D inadequacy compared to outdoor sport athletes.
Athletes training at latitudes above 40°N have a significantly higher risk of vitamin D inadequacy.
specific_value: RR 1.85 (95% CI 1.35-2.53) after excluding Middle East outlier

text: The prevalence of injuries among vitamin D inadequate athletes is notable, with muscle and soft-tissue injuries at 37.5% and bone-related injuries at 19%.
specific_value: injuries 43% (95% CI 20-68%); bone 19% (95% CI 7-36%); muscle/soft-tissue 37.5% (95% CI 11.5-68.5%)

text: Regular monitoring of serum 25(OH)D levels and vitamin D supplementation is recommended for athletes, especially those training indoors, at high latitudes, or during winter/spring.
